rationale: The cited paper is discussed with a specific methodological role, not merely as background. In developing the model, the authors explicitly adopt a response-choice stage for nonignorable missing ratings "as Ying, Feinberg, and Wedel (2006) suggest" and present the corresponding conditional formulation. They also implement Ying et al. as a distinct benchmark model in the empirical comparison and show improvements over it. This shows the citation meaningfully informs both model specification and positioning of contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Ying et al.'s two-stage missing-rating treatment (selection plus rating) as a component within its own framework for recommendation data. It extends that line by adding a virtual-expert latent residual structure and demonstrating superior predictive performance relative to the Ying et al. model.
